definitions.definition:
To intend.
  1. To intend, to plan (to do); to have as one's intention.

parsedExamples.example: I didn't mean to knock your tooth out.
I mean to go to Arévalo in Spain this summer; I’ve been meaning to tell you for weeks, but I’ve just found the time.
I meant to take the car in for a smog check, but it slipped my mind.
The authors meant a challenge to the status quo.
Do you mean to say you regret it after all?
Don't be angry; she meant well.
Actually this desk was meant for the subeditor.
Man was not meant to question such things.
Your reasoning seems needlessly abstruse, complex, and verbose for me. I mean, could you dumb it down for my sake?

examples: I didn't mean to knock your tooth out.
I mean to go to Arévalo in Spain this summer; I’ve been meaning to tell you for weeks, but I’ve just found the time.
I meant to take the car in for a smog check, but it slipped my mind.
The authors meant a challenge to the status quo.
Do you mean to say you regret it after all?
Don't be angry; she meant well.
Actually this desk was meant for the subeditor.
Man was not meant to question such things.
Your reasoning seems needlessly abstruse, complex, and verbose for me. I mean, could you dumb it down for my sake?

definitions.definition: To intend, to plan (to do); to have as one's intention.

parsedExamples.example: I didn't mean to knock your tooth out.
I mean to go to Arévalo in Spain this summer; I’ve been meaning to tell you for weeks, but I’ve just found the time.
I meant to take the car in for a smog check, but it slipped my mind.
The authors meant a challenge to the status quo.
Do you mean to say you regret it after all?

examples: I didn't mean to knock your tooth out.
I mean to go to Arévalo in Spain this summer; I’ve been meaning to tell you for weeks, but I’ve just found the time.
I meant to take the car in for a smog check, but it slipped my mind.
The authors meant a challenge to the status quo.
Do you mean to say you regret it after all?

definitions.definition: To have as intentions of a given kind.

parsedExamples.example: Don't be angry; she meant well.

examples: Don't be angry; she meant well.

definitions.definition: To intend (something) for a given purpose or fate; to predestine.

parsedExamples.example: Actually this desk was meant for the subeditor.
Man was not meant to question such things.

examples: Actually this desk was meant for the subeditor.
Man was not meant to question such things.

definitions.definition: To intend an ensuing comment or statement as an explanation.

parsedExamples.example: Your reasoning seems needlessly abstruse, complex, and verbose for me. I mean, could you dumb it down for my sake?

examples: Your reasoning seems needlessly abstruse, complex, and verbose for me. I mean, could you dumb it down for my sake?

definitions.definition:
To convey (a meaning).
  1. To convey (a given sense); to signify, or indicate (an object or idea).

parsedExamples.example: The sky is red this morning—does that mean we're in for a storm?
What does this hieroglyph mean?
I’m afraid I don’t understand what you mean.
He’s a little different, if you know what I mean.

examples: The sky is red this morning—does that mean we're in for a storm?
What does this hieroglyph mean?
I’m afraid I don’t understand what you mean.
He’s a little different, if you know what I mean.

definitions.definition: To convey (a given sense); to signify, or indicate (an object or idea).

parsedExamples.example: The sky is red this morning—does that mean we're in for a storm?

examples: The sky is red this morning—does that mean we're in for a storm?

definitions.definition: Of a word, symbol etc: to have reference to, to signify.

parsedExamples.example: What does this hieroglyph mean?

examples: What does this hieroglyph mean?

definitions.definition: Of a person (or animal etc): to intend to express, to imply, to hint at, to allude.

parsedExamples.example: I’m afraid I don’t understand what you mean.
He’s a little different, if you know what I mean.

examples: I’m afraid I don’t understand what you mean.
He’s a little different, if you know what I mean.

definitions.definition: To have conviction in (something said or expressed); to be sincere in (what one says).

parsedExamples.example: Does she really mean what she said to him last night?
Say what you mean and mean what you say.

examples: Does she really mean what she said to him last night?
Say what you mean and mean what you say.

definitions.definition: To cause or produce (a given result); to bring about (a given result).

parsedExamples.example: One faltering step means certain death.
This breakthrough will mean that we spend less on electricity bills.

examples: One faltering step means certain death.
This breakthrough will mean that we spend less on electricity bills.

definitions.definition: To be of some level of importance.

parsedExamples.example: That little dog meant everything to me.
Formality and titles mean nothing in their circle.

examples: That little dog meant everything to me.
Formality and titles mean nothing in their circle.

definitions.definition: To lament.
Common; general.
Of a common or low origin, grade, or quality; common; humble.

parsedExamples.example: a man of mean parentage
a mean abode

examples: a man of mean parentage
a mean abode

definitions.definition: Low in quality or degree; inferior; poor; shabby.

parsedExamples.example: a mean appearance
a mean dress

examples: a mean appearance
a mean dress

definitions.definition: Without dignity of mind; destitute of honour; low-minded; spiritless; base.

parsedExamples.example: a mean motive
It was mean of you to steal that little girl's piggy bank.

examples: a mean motive
It was mean of you to steal that little girl's piggy bank.

definitions.definition: Of little value or worth; worthy of little or no regard; contemptible; despicable.
Ungenerous; stingy; tight-fisted.

parsedExamples.example: He's so mean. I've never seen him spend so much as five pounds on presents for his children.

examples: He's so mean. I've never seen him spend so much as five pounds on presents for his children.

definitions.definition: Disobliging; pettily offensive or unaccommodating.
Intending to cause harm, successfully or otherwise; bearing ill will towards another.

parsedExamples.example: Watch out for her: she's mean. I said good morning to her, and she punched me in the nose.

examples: Watch out for her: she's mean. I said good morning to her, and she punched me in the nose.

definitions.definition: Powerful; fierce; strong.

parsedExamples.example: It must have been a mean typhoon that levelled this town.

examples: It must have been a mean typhoon that levelled this town.

definitions.definition: Hearty; spicy.
Accomplished with great skill; deft; hard to compete with.

parsedExamples.example: Your mother can roll a mean cigarette.
He hits a mean backhand.

examples: Your mother can roll a mean cigarette.
He hits a mean backhand.

definitions.definition: Difficult, tricky.

parsedExamples.example: This problem is mean!

examples: This problem is mean!

definitions.definition: Having the mean as its value; average.

parsedExamples.example: The mean family has 2.4 children.

examples: The mean family has 2.4 children.

definitions.definition: Middling; intermediate; moderately good, tolerable.
A method or course of action used to achieve some result.
An intermediate step or intermediate steps.
Something which is intermediate or in the middle; an intermediate value or range of values; a medium.
The middle part of three-part polyphonic music; now specifically, the alto part in polyphonic music; an alto instrument.
Any function of multiple variables that satisfies certain properties and yields a number representative of its arguments; or, the number so yielded; a measure of central tendency.
The average of a set of values, calculated by summing them together and dividing by the number of terms.
Either of the two numbers in the middle of a conventionally presented proportion, as 2 and 3 in 1:2=3:6.
mean (unkind; offensive)
to be mean towards someone
centre, middle

parsedExamples.example: Share çhyndaa cabbil ayns mean ny h-aah na goll er vaih.
parsedExamples.translation: Better to change horses in mid ford than to drown.

examples: Share çhyndaa cabbil ayns mean ny h-aah na goll er vaih.

definitions.definition: interior

parsedExamples.example: Tar stiagh ayns mean y killagh.
parsedExamples.translation: Come into the body of the church.

examples: Tar stiagh ayns mean y killagh.

definitions.definition: average

parsedExamples.example: Trogmayd mean.

examples: Trogmayd mean.

definitions.definition: little, tiny
third-person plural present indicative of mear
red
